content:
# Final Review Proposal
**Producer:** agent:codex/gpt-5.4-xhigh

## Summary

Reviewed the milestone lifecycle/journal changes and found 2 correctness issues, both around resumed-at handling: one breaks exact-lineage matching after resume, and one mis-timestamps paused -> running bridge events during repair, which also undercounts duration metadata.

## Amendments

1. Fix resumed-at timestamp consistency for milestone lineage. In src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs:1619-1654, resume writes the durable run_resumed event at one Utc::now() and then calls sync_milestone_bead_start with a second Utc::now(). Later, src/cli/run.rs:226-243 derives the canonical attempt start from the run_resumed timestamp, and src/cli/run.rs:489-519 gates failed-resume repair on an exact lineage match. Those timestamps no longer line up, so resumed attempts can look like "missing lineage" even when start sync already happened; on a failed resume that can make sync_terminal_milestone_task_with_options return early and leave the milestone in the wrong lifecycle state. Capture one resumed-at timestamp and use it consistently for the run_resumed journal row, the milestone start sync, and the exact-lineage check.
   Rationale: The current code creates two different start times for the same resumed attempt. That breaks the new exact-match repair logic and can suppress terminal milestone reconciliation after a failed resume.
2. Preserve the known resumed-at time when synthesizing paused -> running bridge events. build_reconciled_transition_events in src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:821-830 ignores the supplied running_started_at whenever from_status == Paused and stamps the synthetic running event with now instead. But both src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:2111-2118 and src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:2239-2246 pass the real finalized/repaired run start time into that helper. In resumed-at repair flows (for example, repairing a stale failed lineage row after a later successful resume), the emitted paused -> running bridge lands at completion time and duration_seconds undercounts the whole resumed segment. Use running_started_at for that synthetic running transition when it is available.
   Rationale: This is a correctness bug in the new lifecycle journaling: the bridge event timestamp is wrong, and completed/failed metadata can report a shorter runtime than actually occurred.
